perturbation.original_text: 10. Any dispute, controv ersy or claim arising out of or relating to this Agreement or any related agreement or other document or the validity, interpretation, breach or termination thereof ( Dispute'), including claims seeking redress or asserting rights under applicable law, shal l, be resolved amicably between the parties and in case the dispute could not be settled by mutual negotiation, then the same shall be referred to a sole arbitrator appointed by the Bank and the decision of the Arbitrator shall be final and bi nding on the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Neither party shall make news releases, public announcement s, give interviews, issue or publish advertisements or publicize in any other manner whatsoever in connectio n with this Agreement , the content s / provision s thereof , other information relating to this Agreement, the Purpose, the Confidential Information or other matter of this Agreement, without the prior written approval of the other party. 10. Any dispute, controv ersy or claim arising out of or relating to this Agreement or any related agreement or other document or the validity, interpretation, breach or termination thereof ( Dispute'), including claims seeking redress or asserting rights under applicable law, shal l, be resolved amicably between the parties and in case the dispute could not be settled by mutual negotiation, then the same shall be referred to a sole arbitrator appointed by the Bank and the decision of the Arbitrator shall be final and bi nding on the parties.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the arbitration clause into the section about publicity makes it less prominent and potentially overlooked. Arbitration clauses are required to be clear and conspicuous.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 7(3) of the Indian Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996 requires an arbitration agreement to be in writing. While this is satisfied, the clause being buried within publicity restrictions impairs its visibility and thus enforceability, acting against the spirit of clear agreement.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 39 CFR § 7.3 - Exceptions. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 39 CFR § 7.3 - Exceptions. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 7.3 Exceptions. Section 7.2 of these bylaws does not apply to a portion of a meeting , and §§ 7.4 and 7.5 do not apply to information concerning the meeting which otherwise would be required to be disclosed to the public, if the Board properly determines that the public interest does not require otherwise, and that such portion of the meeting or the disclosure of such information is likely to: (a) Disclose matters that are: (1) Specifically authorized under criteria established by an Executive order to be kept secret in the interests of national defense or foreign policy, and (2) In fact properly classified under that Executive order; (b) Relate solely to the internal personnel rules and practices of the Postal Service , including the Postal Service position in negotiations or consultations with employee organizations. (c) Disclose matters specifically exempted from disclosure by statute (other than the Freedom of Information Act , 5 U.S.C. 552 ), provided that the statute: (1) Requires that the matters be withheld from the public in such a manner as to leave no discretion on the issue, or (2) Establishes particular criteria for withholding or refers to particular types of matters to be withheld; (d) Disclose trade secrets and commercial or financial information obtained from a person and privileged or confidential, such as market information pertinent to Postal Service borrowing or investments, technical or patent information related to postal mechanization, or commercial information related to purchases of real estate; (e) Involve accusing any person of a crime, or formally censuring any person ; (f) Disclose information of a personal nature, such as personal or medical data regarding any individual if disclosure would constitute a clearly u
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that the arbitration clause was moved to a section about publicity, making it less prominent and potentially overlooked. Arbitration clauses are required to be clear and conspicuous. The law explanation further states that section 7(3) of the Indian Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996 requires an arbitration agreement to be in writing, and while this is satisfied, the clause being buried impairs its visibility and thus enforceability. While scraped law snippet 1 is irrelevant, the change in the contract text, combined with the perturbation and law explanations, demonstrates a structural flaw that undermines the enforceability of the arbitration clause. This aligns with the definition of a Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction, where a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret.
